skill.name: marketing-strategy-pmm
skill.description: Product marketing strategy playbook for positioning, ICP definition, GTM planning, competitive intelligence, launches, market entry, sales enablement, and PMM reporting.
skill.summary: Build practical product marketing strategies, launch plans, battlecards, and GTM assets for B2B SaaS teams.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 78 patterns, but review found them in Markdown templates and business guidance, not executable code. No evidence found of prompt injection, network calls, filesystem access, or command execution in the only scanned file. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-source review limits.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command findings occur in Markdown examples and business templates, not Ruby or shell execution. No command invocation or user input execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are fenced Markdown examples, tables, and reporting templates. There is no executable script file and no shell command syntax that would run in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts point to product marketing prose, slide templates, and asset names. No hashing, encryption, password handling, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary marketing content such as descriptions, interview questions, sales deck guidance, and template filenames. No cryptographic operation or security-sensitive data flow is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Matches in Marketing Research
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance alerts describe competitor research, launch awareness work, audience planning, and win/loss interviews. They do not instruct host scanning, port scanning, or unauthorized network probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is product marketing research and campaign planning. No tools, commands, targets, or instructions for technical reconnaissance were found.

content.value_statement: Product marketing teams need clear positioning, launch plans, and sales enablement assets under tight timelines. This skill provides structured PMM frameworks for GTM strategy, ICP work, competitive intelligence, and reporting.
actual_capabilities: Guides ICP definition using firmographic, technographic, psychographic, and persona criteria.
Builds positioning and messaging with an April Dunford-style workflow.
Creates competitive intelligence workflows, battlecard structures, and win/loss analysis questions.
Plans GTM motions, 90-day launches, market entry priorities, and launch tiering.
Defines sales enablement assets, handoff protocols, training plans, and PMM KPI dashboards.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or working automation despite referencing possible script resources.
Requires current market data, customer research, and competitor evidence from the user.
Focuses mainly on B2B SaaS and Series A or later company contexts.
Does not replace legal, finance, privacy, or regional compliance review.

use_cases.title: Define a new product position
use_cases.description: Turn customer segments, alternatives, differentiators, and outcomes into a clear positioning narrative and messaging hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Plan an international GTM motion
use_cases.description: Compare target regions, choose launch priorities, allocate budget, and outline localized campaigns for expansion.

use_cases.title: Prepare sales enablement assets
use_cases.description: Create battlecards, sales deck outlines, demo scripts, handoff rules, and training plans for sales teams.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify ICP
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for defining target customers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to define an ICP for a B2B SaaS product. Ask me for missing context first, then produce firmographic, technographic, psychographic, and persona criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build Positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the positioning framework to compare our alternatives, unique attributes, target buyers, category choice, proof points, and final messaging hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Create Launch Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 90-day GTM launch plan for this product. Include pre-launch, launch, post-launch, channels, metrics, sales enablement, and owners.

prompt_templates.title: Develop Market Entry Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate US, UK, DACH, France, and Canada for market entry. Score each market, recommend sequencing, and produce a budget, localization, and risk plan.

output_examples.input: We need positioning for a workflow automation tool sold to mid-market SaaS operations teams.
output_examples.output: A positioning brief with competitive alternatives, unique attributes, buyer segments, value themes, proof points, category recommendation, and a messaging hierarchy.

output_examples.input: Prepare sales enablement for a launch against two named competitors.
output_examples.output: A battlecard outline, discovery questions, objection responses, demo flow, proof assets, and monthly update process for sales teams.

output_examples.input: Choose the first international market for our Series A SaaS company.
output_examples.output: A market priority matrix with TAM, sales cycle, localization needs, privacy considerations, budget allocation, and recommended launch sequence.

best_practices: Provide customer research, win/loss notes, pricing, and competitor names before asking for final strategy.
Ask for structured outputs such as briefs, scorecards, launch calendars, and battlecard sections.
Validate recommendations with sales, product, customer success, and regional legal stakeholders.
anti_patterns: Do not use the frameworks with generic product details and expect precise market strategy.
Do not treat benchmark targets as guaranteed performance forecasts.
Do not publish competitor claims without current evidence and internal review.

faq.question: What type of marketing work does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports product marketing strategy, positioning, ICP work, GTM planning, launch execution, competitive intelligence, sales enablement, and PMM reporting.

faq.question: Is this skill only for SaaS companies?
faq.answer: It is strongest for B2B SaaS teams, especially Series A and later companies. Other teams can adapt the frameworks.

faq.question: Can it create competitive battlecards?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides battlecard sections for competitor overview, strengths, weaknesses, objection handling, traps, proof points, and sales guidance.

faq.question: Does it collect competitor data automatically?
faq.answer: No. The included file is guidance only. Users must provide current competitor information or connect separate approved research tools.

faq.question: Can it help with international market entry?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers market prioritization, localization, regional channels, legal considerations, budget planning, and launch sequencing.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output quality?
faq.answer: Useful inputs include customer segments, revenue targets, target regions, competitors, sales cycle data, pricing, product differentiators, and launch constraints.
